title: Tea on Linen
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; cold flush + vinegar + 3% peroxide on white linen, structured = pro
stainChemistry: Tea on linen is a tannin stain — black tea harder than green because of more oxidation. The recurring traps: hot water (sets tannin), alkaline cleaners (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone before peroxide all DARKEN tannin permanently), and chlorine bleach (locks azo-type tannin variants). Linen is cellulose (similar to cotton) but more delicate when wet — wrinkles aggressively, structured / lined / vintage / dry-clean-only linen warps under aggressive handling. Tea with milk adds milk protein and milk fat — needs the protein-first order discipline (this card covers black tea; tea-with-milk follows coffee-cream-style ordering).
whyThisWorks: Cold water back-flush extracts bulk tannin before it sets. Diluted vinegar's mild acid keeps tannin on the lighter side and disrupts the fiber grip. 3% peroxide on white linen handles residual brown. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY FLAT (linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet) finishes. Structured / vintage linen narrows the bench sharply.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): tea is theaflavins + thearubigins (oxidized catechin polymers) + tannin polyphenols. Tannin anchors to linen cellulose via hydrogen bonding. Pro uses cold acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂ + acetic acid neutralizing rinse. Sodium hydrosulfite for set chromophore.
defaultAssumption: Tea spill on a simple flat washable linen napkin / shirt / tablecloth, fresh and not yet hot-dried. Black tea is the harder variant; tea with milk needs protein-first order. Structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen = home candidate.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water with a white towel underneath the front. 60–90 sec. Cold only — heat sets tannin into linen permanently.

spottingProtocol.technique: Mild acid keeps tannin on light side
spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, press 5–10 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.
WHITE or light linen only. Hidden test on dyed linen. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. NEVER mix with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Care label triage — structured / vintage = pro.
3. Diluted white vinegar 5–10 min on damp cloth.
4. 3% H₂O₂ for white linen, 5–10 min — hidden test on dyed.
5. Cool wash + AIR-DRY flat. No heat until cleared.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — heat sets tannin into linen
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently
Hidden test on dyed linen before peroxide
Linen air-dries FLAT — hanging stretches the fiber

homeSolutions.agent: Cold and Acid First — No Alkaline Panic
homeSolutions.instruction: Tea on linen is a tannin stain. Two hard rules: NO hot water (sets tannin) and NO alkaline cleaners (baking soda, ammonia, OxiClean alone before peroxide all darken tannin permanently). The path: cold back-flush → vinegar → 3% peroxide on white linen → cool wash + AIR-DRY FLAT. Structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen goes to pro from minute one — wet handling warps these pieces.
Find the care tag. Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one. Simple flat washable linen (napkin, plain shirt, basic tablecloth) = home candidate.
Turn the linen inside out. Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water with a white towel underneath the front. 60 to 90 seconds. Cold only — heat sets tannin into linen permanently.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Apply with a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes. The mild acid keeps tannin on the lighter side. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide — acid plus peroxide degrades the peroxide.
WHITE or very light linen only: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes, blot. Hidden-area test on dyed linen first.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when wet, and hanging it stretches the fiber. Inspect dry before any iron. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water.
3. Diluted white vinegar 5–10 min on damp cloth.
4. 3% peroxide damp cloth on white linen only — hidden test on dyed.
5. Cool wash + AIR-DRY FLAT. No iron until cleared.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen (pro). Stop if hot water or hot dryer was used (often locks tannin). Stop if alkaline cleaner was used as first step (darkened tannin permanently). Stop after 2 home cycles plateau.
warnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently
Linen air-dries FLAT — hanging stretches it

neverDo: Never alkaline cleaners on tannin — darken permanently
Never hot water on tea + linen
Never iron over a brown stain
Never mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia / alkaline
fiberSensitivities: White / light washable linen: tolerates vinegar + 3% peroxide
Dyed linen: hidden test mandatory before peroxide
Structured / lined linen: pro — warps under wet handling
Linen wrinkles aggressively wet — air-dry FLAT

materialWarnings: No hot water — sets tannin into linen
Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one

escalation.when: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen. Hot water or hot dryer already used. Alkaline cleaner used as first step (darkened tannin). 2 home cycles plateau. Hidden test fails on dyed linen.
escalation.whatToTell: "Tea on [white / dyed / structured / vintage] linen, [black / green / herbal / with milk], age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [cold back-flush + diluted vinegar + 3% peroxide on white + cool wash + air-dry flat]. No hot, no alkaline. [Residual brown / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with linen experience plus tannin-spotter chemistry

contextQuestions: Black tea, green tea, or herbal?
With milk (adds protein step) or black tea only?
Simple flat linen, structured / lined, or vintage?
Has alkaline cleaner been used as first step?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Black tea has the most tannin — hardest variant. Tea with milk adds the milk-protein step (use coffee-cream protocol order: protein FIRST, tannin SECOND). The most common home failure is reaching for OxiClean alone or baking soda first — alkaline darkens tannin permanently. Linen-specific: wet handling on structured / lined linen warps the piece — pro from minute one.
